user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Blue Yonder Airlines. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 15:35 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 16:40 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 14:49 EST
Subject: URGENT: QBR preparation Issue at Blue Yonder Airlines

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-01-01
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is QBR preparation.

[00:06:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:09:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:14:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:22:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:26:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:30:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:35:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:40:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.
